summary_en: FY3/26 year-end dividend raised to 66 yen/share from 55 yen/share prior forecast: 61 yen ordinary dividend (6 yen increase) + 5 yen commemorative dividend marking 60th anniversary. Payout-ratio target 30–40% range.
title_en: Notice Regarding Revision to Dividend Forecast for the Fiscal Year Ended March 2026 (Dividend Increase and Commemorative Dividend)
translation_en_brief:
Showa System Engineering announced on 14 May 2026 a revision to its dividend forecast for the fiscal year ended March 2026. The year-end dividend per share has been increased to 66 yen from the prior forecast of 55 yen, comprising 61 yen in ordinary dividend (a 6 yen increase) and 5 yen in commemorative dividend.

The company cited its dividend policy framework, which targets a payout ratio of 30–40% while maintaining adequate shareholder equity and earnings capacity. The increase was also motivated by the company's 60th anniversary on 7 April 2026, with the 5 yen commemorative dividend reflecting gratitude to shareholders and stakeholders for their support.

No interim dividend (Q2) is scheduled. The revised forecast reflects current-period performance assessed against the company's medium-term capital allocation principles.
